behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: failure appears at real filesystem watcher boundary (recursive watch / subdirectory event emission). MOCK-HIDE/REAL-INFRA: in-process event stubs can pass even if OS watcher never emits subdirectory events. TWO-PASS down-check (Integration) rejected by behavior text: it requires a real file being placed in a real subdirectory and being detected while daemon runs. Higher-level (Agentic/Workflow) rejected: no AI/ML call or multi-agent steps in behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real temp input/output directory. Create a nested subdirectory under input, then write a file into that subdirectory. Assert transformed output appears once in output directory for that file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: this behavior is about dedupe decision logic (same path + time window) and can fail inside one local component with a controllable clock. MOCK-MISS says Unit is valid because failure does not require module handoff by description. TWO-PASS up-check (Integration) considered for queue/watcher wiring, but rejected by behavior text focusing on deduplication logic itself, not event ingestion. Higher levels rejected: no OS/network/subprocess requirement and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedupe component with a fake clock and configured window. Submit request for path P at t0, then submit P again at t0+delta (delta < window). Assert second request is rejected; then at t0+window+epsilon assert request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate filesystem events. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): yes, real OS event behavior is required. MOCK-HIDE: manually calling handler twice may not reproduce watcher coalescing/timing quirks. TWO-PASS down-check (Integration) rejected by explicit behavior text naming OS-emitted multiple events. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched directory. Perform one file write pattern known to trigger multiple events on target OS (e.g., write temp + rename or chunked write). Capture processing side effects and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: requires real directory delete/recreate OS events and watcher reattachment behavior. REAL-INFRA is intrinsic; mocks can miss backend-specific watch invalidation semantics. TWO-PASS down-check (Integration) rejected by behavior text requiring live daemon recovery across real directory lifecycle changes. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory. Remove the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then place a new input file. Assert daemon processes the new file without restarting process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: ordering depends on real file arrival + filesystem timestamp retrieval/resolution under concurrency. REAL-INFRA: creation timestamp precision and event ordering can differ in actual FS/OS. TWO-PASS down-check (Integration) possible by injecting metadata, but rejected as minimum for real-failure detection because fake timestamps hide FS precision/tie behaviors implied by 'arrive at the same time'. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: In a real watched directory, create several files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times (or staggered by minimal interval), then assert processing order matches creation timestamp order observed by daemon logic.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: correctness depends on real write-in-progress behavior and filesystem event timing. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): yes. MOCK-HIDE: fake 'write complete' flags can pass while real partial-write races fail. TWO-PASS down-check (Integration) rejected by behavior text explicitly coupling arrival event timing to ongoing OS-level file writes. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory. Begin writing a large file in chunks with delays so arrival event occurs before final close. Assert daemon does not process partial content and only processes once final write is complete.
